reviews.review_title: Can't wait to see what this becomes
reviews.review_description: I can't wait to see what this game becomes later with some updates.  I would love to see an actual practice level that teaches us to aim bc it doesn't seem exactly like real archery aiming, it is a little bit of a learning curve but so close to being great. I also agree big time with the other commenter that left hand mode would be great, I like to switch hands and was bummed I couldn't.   I think having having differnt modes for beginners and advanced would be helpful too,  like in the beginning I feel like you shouldn't have to get higher than 6 on each target just to move forward if you are new to archery. Maybe having 1 extra arrow on each level to try again would be better.  And lastly I'd love to have a retry button so when you know you won't get the score you need to move on you can just not waste your time and hit retry. Otherwise really enjoying this!

I think this has good bones and could be a very good game. I would like to see an option to select left handed shooting in the future. The mechanics are solid, though, and simulates real barebow shooting.

The mechanic should be simple. Hold bow in one hand, pullback draw string with other, aim then release. However I repeatedly couldn't grab the drawstring and gave up after about 5 minutes of futilely grabbing at thin air. Even for £1.49 the game isn't worth it. It's an  incredibly frustrating experience.

reviews.review_title: Some graphics bugs at the moment
reviews.review_description:
The archery sections of some of theulti sport games I've played are the ones I enjoy most soni was excited to try this out. But it's disappointing. Graphically simple, visually a bit dull and hard to move past levels makes it borining.

There are glitches too. Often you can't grab the bow string. Sometimes the bow disappears.
